16.1: Jesus also said to his disciples: «A rich man had a manager who was accused in his presence of wasting his possessions.
16.2: He called him and said: What is this I hear about you? Give an account of your management: for from now on you will no longer be able to manage my property.
16.3: Then the steward said to himself, "What shall I do, since my master is taking away my management of his property? I am not strong enough to work the land, and I am ashamed to beg.".
16.4: I know what I will do, so that when my job is taken away from me, there will be people who will receive me into their homes.
16.5: So he called in his master's debtors one by one, and said to the first, "How much do you owe my master?"
16.6: He replied: One hundred barrels of oil. The steward told him: Take your note: sit down quickly and write fifty.
16.7: Then he said to another, "And you, how much do you owe?" He replied, "One hundred measures of wheat." The steward told him, "Take your bill and write eighty.".
16.8: And the master praised the dishonest steward for having acted skillfully, for the children of this world are more skillful with each other than the children of light.
16.9: I also tell you: Make friends for yourselves by means of the riches of iniquity, so that when you leave this life, they may receive you into the eternal dwellings.
16.10: He who is faithful in small things is faithful also in great things, and he who is unjust in small things is unjust also in great things.
16.11: If you have not been faithful in the riches obtained through iniquity, who will entrust to you the true riches?
16.12: And if you have not been faithful in something other than what belongs to you, who will give you what is yours?
16.13: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and wealth.»
16.14: The Pharisees, who loved money, also listened to all this and mocked him.
16.15: Jesus said to them, «You are the ones who justify yourselves in the eyes of men, but God knows your hearts, and what is highly valued in the sight of men is an abomination in the sight of God.
16.16: The Law and the Prophets go up to John; since John, the kingdom of God has been proclaimed, and everyone strives to enter it.
16.17: Heaven and earth will pass away more easily than one stroke of the law will perish.
16.18: Anyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and anyone who marries a woman divorced by her husband commits adultery.
16.19: There was a rich man who was dressed in purple and fine linen and who held sumptuous feasts every day.
16.20: A poor man named Lazarus was lying at his gate, covered with sores,
16.21: and wishing to fill himself with the crumbs that fell from the rich man's table, but even the dogs came to lick his ulcers.
16.22: Now it happened that the poor man died and was carried by the angels to Abraham's bosom. The rich man also died and was buried.
16.23: In Hades, where he was in torment, he looked up and saw Abraham far away, with Lazarus by his side.,
16.24: And he cried out, “Father Abraham, have mercy on me and send Lazarus to dip the tip of his finger in water and cool my tongue, because I am in agony from these flames.”.
16.25: Abraham replied: My son, remember that in your lifetime you received your good things, and likewise Lazarus received his bad things: But now he is comforted here, and you are in pain.
16.26: Moreover, between us and you there is forever a great chasm, so that those who would like to pass from here to you cannot do so, and it is impossible to pass from there to us.
16.27: And the rich man said, “Then I beg you, father, send Lazarus to my father’s house,
16.28: for I have five brothers, to testify to these things to them lest they also come to this place of torment.
16.29: Abraham replied: They have Moses and the Prophets; let them listen to them.
16.30: No, Abraham, our father, he replied, but if someone from the dead goes to them, they will repent.
16.31: But Abraham said to him, »If they do not listen to Moses and the Prophets, someone will rise from the dead, yet they will not believe it.”

17.1: Jesus also said to his disciples: «It is impossible that scandals should not come, but woe to him through whom they come.
17.2: It would be better for him to have a millstone put around his neck and be thrown into the sea, than to scandalize one of these little ones.
17.3: Be careful of yourselves. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him.
17.4: And even if he sins against you seven times in a day, and seven times returns to you saying, »I repent,” you must forgive him.”
17.5: The apostles said to the Lord, "Increase our faith."«
17.6: The Lord replied, «If you had faith like a mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, ‘Be uprooted and transplanted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.
17.7: Which of you, having a servant plowing or tending sheep, will say to him, when he comes in from the field, “Come quickly and sit down to eat”?
17.8: Won't he say to him, on the contrary: Prepare my supper, gird yourself and serve me, until I have eaten and drunk, and after that you shall eat and drink?
17.9: Does he show gratitude to this servant because he did what he was ordered to do?
17.10: I don't think so. Likewise, when you have done what you were commanded, say, 'We are unworthy servants; we have only done our duty.'»
17.11: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus was close to the border of Samaria and Galilee.
17.12: As he entered a village, ten lepers came to meet him and, keeping their distance,
17.13: They raised their voices, saying, "Jesus, Master, have mercy on us."«
17.14: As soon as he saw them, he said, "Go, show yourselves to the priests." And as they went, they were healed.
17.15: One of them, when he saw he was healed, retraced his steps, glorifying God aloud,
17.16: And falling face down at Jesus' feet, he gave him thanks. Now he was a Samaritan.
17.17: Then Jesus spoke up and said, «Were not the ten healed? And where are the nine?”
17.18: Was this foreigner the only one among them found to return and give glory to God?
17.19: And he said to him, »Rise and go; your faith has saved you.”
17.20: The Pharisees asked him when the kingdom of God would come, and he answered them, «The kingdom of God does not come in a way that can be observed.
17.21: We will not say, »It is here,” or “It is there,” for behold, the kingdom of God is in your midst.»
17.22: He also said to his disciples, «The time will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.
17.23: You will be told: He is here and: He is there, beware of going there and running after him.
17.24: For as the flash of lightning shines from one end of the sky to the other, so will it be with the Son of Man in his day.
17.25: But first he must suffer greatly and be rejected by this generation.
17.26: And as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be in the days of the Son of Man.
17.27: The men ate and drank, they married and gave in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed them all.
17.28: And as it happened in the days of Lot: people ate and drank, they bought and sold, they planted and built,
17.29: But on the day Lot left Sodom, fire and sulfur rained down from heaven and destroyed them all:
17.30: So it will be on the day the Son of Man appears.
17.31: On that day, let no one who is on the roof and whose belongings are in the house go down to get them, and let no one who is in the field go back.
17.33: Whoever seeks to save their life will lose it, and whoever has lost their life will preserve it.
17.34: I tell you, on that night, of the two people who will be in the same bed, one will be taken and the other left.,
17.35: of two women who will grind together, one will be taken and the other left, [
17.36: of two men who will be in a field, one will be taken and the other left].»
17.37: They asked him, "Where, Lord?" He replied, "Where the body is, there the vultures will gather."«

18.1: He told them another parable, to show that they should always pray and never give up.
18.2: He said: «There was in a certain city a judge who neither feared God nor cared about men.
18.3: There was also a widow in that town who kept coming to him, saying, “Grant me justice against my adversary.”.
18.4: And for a long time he did not want to, but then he said to himself: even if I do not fear God and do not care about men,
18.5: However, because this widow is bothering me, I will give her justice, so that she will not keep coming to torment me.
18.6: "Listen," added the Lord, "to what this unjust judge is saying.".
18.7: And would God not bring justice to his chosen ones who cry out to him night and day, and would he delay in their regard?
18.8: I tell you, he will soon see that they get justice. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on earth?»
18.9: He told this parable again, this time to some people who were convinced of their own perfection and full of contempt for others:
18.10: «Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.
18.11: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself: O God, I thank you that I am not like other men, who are thieves, unjust, and adulterers, nor even like this tax collector.
18.12: I fast twice a week, and I tithe all my income.»
18.13: The tax collector, standing at a distance, would not even lift his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying: O God, have mercy on me, a sinner.
18.14: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other, for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and everyone who humbles himself will be exalted.»
18.15: People also brought their small children to him so that he could touch them, and seeing this, his disciples rebuked them.
18.16: But Jesus called the children and said, «Let the little children come to me and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.
18.17: Truly I tell you, anyone who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.»
18.18: Then a leader asked him, "Good Master, what must I do to obtain eternal life?"«
18.19: Jesus said to him, «Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.”.
18.20: You know the commandments: Do not commit adultery, do not murder, do not steal, do not bear false witness, honor your father and mother.»
18.21: He replied, "I have observed all of this since my youth."«
18.22: Having heard this answer, Jesus said to him, «You still lack one thing: sell everything you have and distribute the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.»
18.23: But when he heard these words, he became sad, because he was very rich.
18.24: Seeing that he had become sad, Jesus said, «How hard it is for those who have riches to enter the kingdom of God.
18.25: Indeed, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.»
18.26: Those who were listening to him said, "Who then can be saved?"«
18.27: He replied, "What is impossible for men is possible for God."«
18.28: Pierre then said, "You see, we left everything and followed you."«
18.29: He said to them, «Truly I tell you, no one will ever leave home or parents or brothers or wife or children for the sake of the kingdom of God,
18.30: without receiving much more in this age or in the age to come eternal life.»
18.31: Then Jesus took the Twelve aside and said to them, «We are going up to Jerusalem, and everything that the prophets have written about the Son of Man will be fulfilled.
18.32: He will be handed over to the pagans and mocked and insulted and spat upon,
18.33: and after they have flogged him, they will put him to death, and he will rise again on the third day.»
18.34: But they understood nothing of it; it was a hidden language to them, the meaning of which they did not grasp.
18.35: As Jesus approached Jericho, it happened that a blind man was sitting by the roadside, begging for alms.
18.36: Hearing many people passing by, he asked what it was.
18.37: They told him, "Jesus of Nazareth is passing by."«
18.38: Immediately he cried out, "Jesus, son of David, have mercy on me."«
18.39: Those who were walking in front rebuked him to silence him, but he shouted all the louder: «Son of David, have mercy on me!»
18.40: Then Jesus stopped and ordered that he be brought to him, and when the blind man approached, he asked him:
18.41: «"What do you want me to do for you?" He said, "Lord, let me see."»
18.42: And Jesus said to him, «See, your faith has saved you.»
18.43: At once he saw him and followed him, glorifying God. And all the people, seeing this, gave praise to God.

19.1: Jesus having entered Jericho, was passing through the city.
19.2: And there was a man named Zacchaeus, a chief tax collector and wealthy,
19.3: He was trying to see who Jesus was, but he couldn't because of the crowd, since he was short in stature.
19.4: Running ahead, he climbed a sycamore tree to see it, because he had to pass by there.
19.5: When Jesus arrived at that place, he looked up and, seeing him, said to him, «Zacchaeus, come down quickly, for I must stay at your house today.»
19.6: Zacchaeus hurried downstairs and received him with joy.
19.7: Seeing this, they all murmured, saying, "He has gone to stay with a sinner."«
19.8: But Zacchaeus stood before the Lord and said, «Look, Lord, I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have cheated anybody out of anything, I will pay back four times the amount.»
19.9: Jesus said to him, «Today salvation has come to this house, because this man, too, is a son of Abraham.
19.10: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save what was lost.»
19.11: As they listened to this speech, he added a parable, because he was near Jerusalem and the people thought that the kingdom of God was about to appear.
19.12: He said, "A man of noble birth went to a distant country to be invested with the kingship and then return.
19.13: Having called ten of his servants, he gave them ten minas and said to them: Put them to good use until I return.
19.14: But his fellow citizens hated him, and they sent messengers after him saying, "We do not want this man to rule over us."»
19.15: When he returned, after being invested with the kingship, he summoned the servants to whom he had given the money, to find out what profit each had made.
19.16: The first one came and said: Lord, your mine has gained ten more mines.
19.17: He said to him, “Well done, good servant; because you have been faithful over a little, you shall have authority over ten cities.”.
19.18: The second one came and said: Lord, your mine has produced five more mines.
19.19: "You too," he told him, "govern five cities.".
19.20: Then another came and said, "Lord, here is your mina, which I have kept in a cloth as a deposit.".
19.21: Because I was afraid of you, because you are a hard man: you take what you did not deposit and you reap what you did not sow.
19.22: The king replied, “I will judge you by your own words, you wicked servant! You knew that I am a strict man, taking what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow.”,
19.23: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? And when I returned, I would have withdrawn it with the interest.
19.24: And he said to those who were there, “Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has ten.”.
19.25: "Sir," they said to him, "he has ten.".
19.26: I tell you, to everyone who has, more will be given, and from him who does not have, even what he has will be taken away.
19.27: As for those people who hate me and refused to have me as their king, bring them here and slaughter them in my presence.»
19.28: After this speech, Jesus began to walk ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
19.29: When he approached Bethphage and Bethany, at the mountain called Olivet, Jesus sent two of his disciples,
19.30: saying: "Go to the village opposite, when you enter it, you will find a donkey tied up, on which no man has ever sat, untie it and bring it here.
19.31: And if someone asks you why you are untying it, you will answer: Because the Lord needs it.»
19.32: Those who were sent went and found things just as Jesus had told them.
19.33: As they were untying the donkey, its owners said to them, "Why are you untying this donkey?"«
19.34: They replied, "Because the Lord needs it."«
19.35: And they brought it to Jesus, and then, having thrown their cloaks over the donkey, they made Jesus get on it.
19.36: As he passed by, people spread their coats on the road.
19.37: When he was already near the descent from the Mount of Olives, the whole crowd of disciples, filled with joy, began to praise God aloud for all the miracles they had seen.
19.38: «Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord,» they said. “Peace in heaven and glory in the highest.”
19.39: Then some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, «Teacher, rebuke your disciples.»
19.40: He replied, "I tell you, if they remain silent, the stones will cry out."«
19.41: And when he drew near and saw Jerusalem, he wept over it, saying:
19.42: «If you, too, only knew on this day that would bring you peace. But now these things are hidden from your eyes.”.
19.43: Days will come upon you when your enemies will surround you with trenches, besiege you, and press in on you from all sides.,
19.44: They will overthrow you and the children in your womb, and they will not leave one stone upon another within your walls, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation.»
19.45: Having entered the temple, he began to drive out those who were buying and selling there,
19.46: saying to them, "It is written: 'My house is a house of prayer,' and you have made it 'a den of robbers.'"«
19.47: Jesus spent his days teaching in the temple. And the chief priests, the scribes, and the notables sought to destroy him.,
19.48: But they did not know how to proceed, for all the people were listening, hanging on his every word.

20.1: One day, as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests and the scribes arrived with the elders,
20.2: and said to him, «Tell us by what authority you are doing these things, or who gave you this authority?»
20.3: Jesus answered them, «I too have a question for you. Answer me.”.
20.4: Was John's baptism from heaven or from men?»
20.5: But they were discussing this among themselves: "If we answer, 'From heaven,' he will say to us, 'Why did you not believe in him?'"
20.6: And if we answer, "Men," the whole crowd will stone us, because they are convinced that John was a prophet.»
20.7: So they replied that they didn't know where he was from.
20.8: «And I,» Jesus said to them, “do not tell you by what authority I do these things.”
20.9: Then he began to tell the people this parable: «A man planted a vineyard and rented it to tenants, and then he went away for a long time to a foreign country.
20.10: When the season came, he sent a servant to the tenants to give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But they beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
20.11: He sent yet another servant, but, having beaten him also and treated him unworthily, they sent him away empty-handed.
20.12: He sent a third, but the tenants also wounded him and threw him out.
20.13: Then the owner of the vineyard said to himself, »What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps when they see him, they will respect him.”
20.14: But when the tenants saw him, they said to one another, "This is the heir; let us kill him, so that the inheritance may be ours.".
20.15: And having thrown him out of the vineyard, they killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
20.16: He will come and destroy those tenants and give his vineyard to others.» When they heard this, they said, «God forbid!»
20.17: But, looking intently at them, Jesus said, «What then is this saying in Scripture: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?’”
20.18: Anyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, and anyone on whom it falls will be crushed.»
20.19: The chief priests and the scribes sought to seize him at once, but fear of the people held them back, for they understood that it was against them that Jesus had told this parable.
20.20: They therefore kept a close watch on him and sent spies pretending to be just, to catch him in his words, in order to deliver him to the authority and power of the governor.
20.21: These people questioned him in these terms: «Teacher, we know that you speak and teach with integrity and impartiality, but that you teach the way of God in truth.
20.22: Are we permitted, or not, to pay tribute to Caesar?»
20.23: Jesus, knowing their deceitfulness, said to them, «Why are you tempting me?
20.24: "Show me a denarius. Whose image and name does it bear?" They answered him, "Caesar's."»
20.25: And he said to them, «Give back to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.»
20.26: Thus they could not catch him in any of his words before the people, and admiring his response, they remained silent.
20.27: Some of the Sadducees, who deny the resurrection, then approached him and questioned him:
20.28: «Teacher,” they said to him, “Moses gave us this law: If a man dies and has no children, his brother must take his wife and raise up children for his brother.
20.29: Now there were seven brothers, the first one took a wife and died childless.
20.30: The second man took his wife and also died childless.,
20.31: The third one then took her, and so did all seven of them, and they died without leaving any children.
20.32: After all of them, the woman also died.
20.33: Whose wife, then, will she be at the time of the resurrection, since she was wife of all seven?»
20.34: Jesus said to them, «The children of this world marry and are given in marriage,
20.35: But those who are found worthy to share in the age to come and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.,
20.36: They can no longer die, since they are like the angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.
20.37: But that the dead are raised, this is what Moses himself made known in the passage of the Burning Bush, when he names the Lord: The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
20.38: But he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive in his sight.»
20.39: Some of the scribes, taking the floor, said to him, "Master, you have spoken well."«
20.40: And they no longer dared to ask him any questions.
20.41: Jesus said to them, «How can anyone say that the Christ is the son of David?
20.42: David himself says in the book of Psalms: The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,”,
20.43: until I make your enemies the footstool for your feet.
20.44: David calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?»
20.45: While all the people were listening to him, he said to his disciples:
20.46: «Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, who like to be greeted in public squares, to occupy the best seats in the synagogues and the best places at banquets:
20.47: Those who devour widows' houses and make a show of long prayers will suffer a more severe condemnation.»

21.1: Jesus, looking up, saw the rich putting their offerings into the offering box.
21.2: He also saw a very poor widow who put two small coins in it.,
21.3: And he said, «I tell you truly, this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
21.4: For all these people gave out of their abundance as an offering to God, but this woman out of her poverty gave everything she had to live on.»
21.5: Some said that the temple was adorned with beautiful stones and rich offerings, Jesus said:
21.6: «"The days will come when, of all that you are looking at here, not one stone will be left upon another that will not be overturned."»
21.7: So they asked him, «Teacher, when will these things happen, and what will be the sign that they are about to take place?»
21.8: Jesus replied, «Beware lest you be deceived, for many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ and the time is near.’ Do not follow them.”.
21.9: And when you hear of wars and revolts, do not be afraid; these things must happen first, but the end will not come soon.»
21.10: He then said to them, «Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
21.11: There will be great earthquakes, plagues and famines in various places, and in the sky terrifying apparitions and extraordinary signs.
21.12: But before all this, they will lay hands on you and persecute you, dragging you into synagogues and prisons, and bringing you before kings and governors because of my name.
21.13: This will happen to you, so that you may bear witness to me.
21.14: Therefore, make it clear to yourselves not to think about your defense in advance.,
21.15: for I myself will give you a mouth and wisdom to which all your enemies will neither be able to respond nor resist.
21.16: You will be betrayed even by your parents, by your brothers, by your relatives and by your friends, and they will put many of you to death.
21.17: You will be hated by everyone because of my name.
21.18: However, not a single hair from your head will be lost.,
21.19: By your steadfastness, you will save your souls.
21.20: But when you see armies besieging Jerusalem, know that its desolation is near.
21.21: Those who are in Judea should flee to the mountains, those who are in the city should leave it, and those who are in the countryside should not enter the city.
21.22: For these will be days of punishment, for the fulfillment of all that is written.
21.23: Woe to those who are pregnant or nursing in those days, for there will be great distress on the earth and great wrath against that people.
21.24: They will fall by the edge of the sword, they will be taken captive among all nations, and Jerusalem will be trampled underfoot by the Gentiles until the times of the Gentiles are fulfilled.
21.25: And there will be signs in the sun, moon, and stars, and on the earth nations will be in anguish and dismay at the roaring and tossing of the sea.,
21.26: Men wither with fear, awaiting what is to come upon the whole earth, for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
21.27: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud with great power and great glory.
21.28: When these things begin to happen, stand up straight and lift up your heads, because your deliverance is drawing near.»
21.29: And he told them this comparison: «Look at the fig tree and all the trees:
21.30: As soon as they start to grow, you know by yourselves, just by seeing them, that summer is near.
21.31: Likewise, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.
21.32: I tell you truly, this generation will not pass away until everything is accomplished.
21.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
21.34: Be careful, or your hearts will be weighed down with carousing, drunkenness and the anxieties of life, and that day will close on you suddenly like a trap.,
21.35: for it will come like a net upon all those who inhabit the face of the whole earth.
21.36: Therefore, be on your guard and pray that you may be found worthy to escape all these evils that are going to happen, and to stand before the Son of Man.
21.37: During the day, Jesus taught in the temple, and he would leave it to spend the night on the mountain called Olivet.
21.38: And all the people, from early morning, came to him in the temple to listen to him.

22.1: The Feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called Passover, was approaching,
22.2: And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how they might put Jesus to death, for they feared the people.
22.3: Then Satan entered into Judas, called Iscariot, one of the Twelve,
22.4: and he went to consult with the Princes of the priests and the magistrates on how to hand him over to them.
22.5: They, filled with joy, promised to give him money.
22.6: He took matters into his own hands and was looking for a favorable opportunity to hand Jesus over to them without the crowd knowing.
22.7: Then came the day of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed.
22.8: Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, «Go and prepare the Passover meal for us.»
22.9: They said to him, "Where would you like us to prepare it?"«
22.10: He answered them, «When you enter the city, you will meet a man carrying a jug of water; follow him into the house he enters,
22.11: and you will say to the master of this house: The Teacher says to you: Where is the room where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?
22.12: And he will show you a large, furnished chamber: prepare what is needed there.»
22.13: They went and found things just as he had told them, and they prepared for Passover.
22.14: When the hour had come, Jesus and the twelve Apostles sat down to eat,
22.15: And he said to them, «I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
22.16: For I tell you, I will not eat it again until the Passover is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.»
22.17: And taking a cup, he gave thanks and said, «Take and share among yourselves.
22.18: For I tell you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.»
22.19: Then he took bread, and having given thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, «This is my body, which is given for you: do this in remembrance of me.»
22.20: He did the same for the cup after supper, saying, «This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you.
22.21: However, here is the hand of the one who betrayed me, with me at this table.
22.22: As for the Son of Man, he will go according to what has been decreed, but woe to the man by whom he is betrayed!»
22.23: And the disciples began to ask one another which of them should do this.
22.24: A dispute also arose among them as to which of them should be considered the greatest.
22.25: Jesus said to them, «The kings of the nations rule over them, and those who command them are called Benefactors.
22.26: But you should not do so, but let the greatest among you be like the least, and the one who rules like the one who serves.
22.27: For who is greater, the one who sits at the table or the one who serves? Is it not the one who sits at the table? But I am among you as one who serves.
22.28: You have remained with me in my trials,
22.29: And I am preparing a kingdom for you, just as my Father has prepared it for me.,
22.30: so that you may eat and drink at my table in my kingdom and sit on thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.»
22.31: And the Lord said, «Simon, Simon, behold, Satan has asked to sift you like wheat,
22.32: But I have prayed for you, that your faith may not fail, and when you have turned again, strengthen your brothers.
22.33: »Lord,” Peter said, “I am ready to go with you both to prison and to death.”
22.34: Jesus answered him, «I tell you, Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you have denied three times that you know me.»
22.35: He also said to his disciples, «When I sent you out without purse, bag, or sandals, did you lack anything?» “Nothing,” they said to him.
22.36: He added: «But now, let him who has a purse take it, and likewise the one who has a bag, and let him who has no sword sell his cloak and buy one.
22.37: For I tell you, this Scripture must still be fulfilled in me: »He was numbered with the transgressors.” For what concerns me is drawing to a close.»
22.38: They said to him, "Lord, there are two swords here." He replied, "That is enough."«
22.39: Having gone out, he went, according to his custom, to the Mount of Olives, and his disciples followed him.
22.40: When he arrived at that place, he said to them, «Pray that you will not fall into temptation.»
22.41: Then he moved away from them to a distance of a stone's throw, and having knelt down, he prayed,
22.42: saying, «Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me. Yet not my will, but yours be done.»
22.43: Then an angel appeared to him from heaven and strengthened him.
22.44: And being in agony, he prayed more earnestly, and his sweat became like drops of blood flowing to the ground.
22.45: After praying, he got up and came to the disciples, whom he found asleep from sorrow.
22.46: And he said to them, «Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray, so that you will not fall into temptation.»
22.47: While he was still speaking, a group of people appeared, with the one called Judas, one of the Twelve, leading the way. He approached Jesus to kiss him.
22.48: And Jesus said to him, «Judas, you are betraying the Son of Man with a kiss.»
22.49: Those who were with Jesus, seeing what was going to happen, said to him, «Lord, should we strike with the sword?»
22.50: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his right ear.
22.51: But Jesus said, «Stay there.» And he touched the man’s ear and healed him.
22.52: Then, addressing the Princes of the priests, the officers of the temple, and the Elders who had come to take him, he said to them: «You have come as after a robber, with swords and clubs.
22.53: »I was with you in the temple every day, and you did not lay a hand on me. But this is your hour, and the power of darkness.”
22.54: Having seized him, they led him away and brought him into the house of the high priest, Peter following at a distance.
22.55: Having lit a fire in the middle of the courtyard, they sat around it and Peter sat among them.
22.56: A servant girl, who saw him sitting by the fire, stared at him intently and said, "This man was also with him."«
22.57: But Peter denied Jesus, saying, "Woman, I do not know him."«
22.58: Shortly after, another man saw him and said, "You are one of them." Peter replied, "Man, I am not."«
22.59: An hour had passed when another man began to say confidently, "Surely this man was with him, for he is from Galilee."«
22.60: Peter replied, "Man, I don't know what you mean." And immediately, while he was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
22.61: And the Lord turned and looked at Peter, and Peter remembered the word that the Lord had spoken to him: «Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.»
22.62: And having left the house, Pierre wept bitterly.
22.63: But those who were holding Jesus mocked him and beat him.
22.64: They blindfolded him and, striking him in the face, questioned him, saying, "Guess who hit you."«
22.65: And they hurled many other insults at him.
22.66: As soon as it was light, the elders of the people, the chief priests, and the scribes gathered together and brought Jesus into their assembly. They said, «If you are the Christ, tell us.»
22.67: He answered them, «If I tell you, you will not believe,
22.68: And if I question you, you will not answer me and you will not release me.
22.69: "From now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of the power of God."»
22.70: Then they all said, "Are you then the Son of God?" He answered them, "You say so, and I am."«
22.71: And they said, "What further need do we have of testimony? We ourselves have heard it from his own lips."«

23.1: Then the whole assembly stood up and led Jesus before Pilate,
23.2: And they began to accuse him, saying, "We have found this man inciting our nation to revolt and forbidding us to pay tribute to Caesar, calling himself Christ the King."«
23.3: Pilate questioned him, saying, "Are you the king of the Jews?" Jesus answered him, "You say so."«
23.4: Pilate said to the chief priests and the people, "I find no guilt in this man."«
23.5: But redoubling their efforts, they said, "He is stirring up the people, spreading his teaching throughout Judea, from Galilee, where he began, even here."«
23.6: When Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.,
23.7: and having learned that he was under Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him back to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
23.8: Herod was overjoyed to see Jesus, for he had long desired to do so, because he had heard much about him and hoped to see him perform some miracle.
23.9: He asked him many questions, but Jesus did not answer him.
23.10: Now, the Princes of the priests and the Scribes were there, accusing him obstinately.
23.11: But Herod, with his guards, treated him with contempt, after mocking him and dressing him in a splendid robe, and sent him back to Pilate.
23.12: On that very day, Herod and Pilate became friends, from having been enemies before.
23.13: Pilate, having assembled the chief priests, the magistrates, and the people,
23.14: He told them: «You brought this man to me as someone inciting the people to revolt; I questioned him in your presence and found none of the crimes you accuse him of,”,
23.15: nor Herod either, for I sent you back to him and, as you see, nothing deserving of death has been proven against him.
23.16: "I will therefore release him after having him punished."»
23.17: [Pilate was obliged, on the day of the festival, to grant them the release of a prisoner].
23.18: But the whole crowd cried out, "Kill this man and release Barabbas to us!"«
23.19: who had been put in prison because of a sedition that had taken place in the city and a murder.
23.20: Pilate, who wished to release Jesus, addressed them again,
23.21: But they responded with this cry: «Crucify him! Crucify him!»
23.22: For the third time, Pilate said to them, «What crime has he committed? I have found nothing in him deserving death. So I will have him punished and send him away.»
23.23: But they persisted, loudly demanding that he be crucified, and their clamor grew louder.
23.24: Pilate therefore declared that it would be done as they asked.
23.25: He released the one they were demanding, who had been put in prison for sedition and murder, and he handed Jesus over to their will.
23.26: As they led him away, they seized a man named Simon, from Cyrene, who was coming in from the country, and they laid the cross on him, to carry it behind Jesus.
23.27: However, he was followed by a large crowd of people and women who beat their breasts and lamented over him.
23.28: Turning to them, Jesus said, «Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children,
23.29: For behold, the days are coming when they will say, “Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.”.
23.30: Then men will begin to say to the mountains: Fall on us, and to the hills: Cover us.
23.31: For if we treat green wood this way, what will we do with dry wood?»
23.32: And two criminals were also brought in to be put to death with Jesus.
23.33: When they arrived at the place called Calvary, they crucified him there, along with the criminals, one on the right, the other on the left.
23.34: But Jesus said, «Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.» Then they divided his clothes among themselves by casting lots.
23.35: The people stood there watching. The rulers joined him in mocking Jesus, saying, "He saved others; let him save himself, if he is the Christ, the chosen one of God."«
23.36: The soldiers also mocked him, approaching and offering him vinegar, saying:
23.37: «"If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself."»
23.38: Above his head was an inscription bearing, in Greek, Latin, and Hebrew characters: "This is the king of the Jews."«
23.39: But one of the criminals who were hanged on the cross was hurling insults at him, saying, "Since you are the Christ, save yourself and save us."«
23.40: But the other rebuked him, saying, "Do you not fear God, you too, condemned as you are to the same punishment?
23.41: "For us, it's justice, because we're receiving what our crimes deserve, but he did nothing wrong."»
23.42: And he said to Jesus, «Lord, remember me when you come into your kingdom.»
23.43: Jesus answered him, «Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.»
23.44: It was about the sixth hour, when darkness covered the whole land until the ninth hour.
23.45: The sun darkened and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
23.46: And Jesus cried out with a loud voice, «Father, into your hands I commit my spirit.» And when he had said this, he breathed his last.
23.47: The centurion, seeing what had happened, glorified God and said, "Surely this man was righteous."«
23.48: And all the multitude that had gathered for this spectacle, considering what had happened, went back beating their chests.
23.49: But all of Jesus' friends stood at a distance, along with the women who had followed him from Galilee, and watched all these things.
23.50: Now there was a man named Joseph, a member of the council, a good and righteous man,
23.51: who had not given his assent either to the plan of others, nor to their actions, he was from Arimathea, a city of Judea, and he too was waiting for the kingdom of God.
23.52: This man went to Pilate and asked him for the body of Jesus.,
23.53: and having taken him down, he wrapped him in a shroud and laid him in a tomb hewn out of the rock, where no one had ever been laid before.
23.54: It was Preparation Day and the Sabbath was about to begin.
23.55: The women who had come from Galilee with Jesus, having accompanied Joseph, considered the tomb and how Jesus’ body had been laid in it.
23.56: So they returned home and prepared spices and perfumes, and on the Sabbath day they rested, according to the commandment.

24.1: But on the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they went to the tomb with the spices they had prepared.
24.2: They saw that the stone had been rolled away from the tomb,
24.3: And when they went in, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
24.4: While they were filled with anxiety about this, two men, dressed in resplendent robes, appeared standing beside them.
24.5: As they bowed their faces to the ground in their terror, the men said to them, «Why do you look for the living among the dead?
24.6: He is not here, but he has risen. Remember what he told you while he was still in Galilee:
24.7: "The Son of Man must be delivered into the hands of sinners, be crucified, and on the third day rise again."»
24.8: They then remembered the words of Jesus,
24.9: And when they returned from the tomb, they reported all these things to the Eleven and to all the others.
24.10: Those who told these things to the Apostles were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and their other companions.
24.11: But they regarded their speeches as delusion and did not believe these women.
24.12: However, Peter got up and ran to the tomb and, having bent down, saw only the linen cloths on the ground and went home, astonished at what had happened.
24.13: Now, on that same day, two disciples were on their way to a village called Emmaus, about sixty stadia from Jerusalem,
24.14: and they talked about all these events.
24.15: While they were conversing and exchanging thoughts, Jesus himself joined them and walked with them.,
24.16: but their eyes were held back so that they did not recognize him.
24.17: He said to them, "What are you discussing as you walk?" Then they stopped, looking very sad.
24.18: One of them, named Cleopas, answered him, "Are you the only foreigner who has come to Jerusalem who does not know the things that have happened there in these days?"
24.19: »What things?« he asked them. They replied, “The things concerning Jesus of Nazareth, who was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
24.20: how the chief priests and our leaders handed him over to be condemned to death and crucified him.
24.21: As for us, we had hoped that he was the one who would deliver Israel, but besides all this, it is now the third day since these things have happened.
24.22: In truth, some of the women who are with us have greatly astonished us: they went to the tomb before daybreak,
24.23: And not having found his body, they came and said that angels had appeared to them and announced that he was alive.
24.24: Some of our people went to the tomb and found everything just as the women had said, but they did not see him.»
24.25: Then Jesus said to them, «O foolish men, and slow of heart to believe all that the prophets have spoken.
24.26: Did not Christ have to suffer all these things in order to enter into his glory?»
24.27: Then, beginning with Moses and going through all the Prophets, he explained to them what was said in all the Scriptures concerning himself.
24.28: When they were near the village they were going to, he pretended to go further.
24.29: But they urged him strongly, saying, «Stay with us, for it is getting late and the day is almost over.» So he went in to stay with them.
24.30: While he was at the table with them, he took the bread, said a blessing, then broke it and gave it to them.
24.31: Then their eyes were opened and they recognized him, but he became invisible to their eyes.
24.32: And they said to one another, «Were not our hearts burning within us while he talked with us on the road and opened the Scriptures to us?»
24.33: Rising at that very hour, they returned to Jerusalem, where they found the Eleven and their companions gathered together,
24.34: who were saying, "The Lord has truly risen and has appeared to Simon."«
24.35: They, in turn, recounted what had happened to them on the way and how they had recognized him in the breaking of the bread.
24.36: While they were talking like this, Jesus appeared among them and said, «Peace be with you. It is I; do not be afraid.»
24.37: Seized with astonishment and terror, they thought they were seeing a spirit.
24.38: But he said to them, «Why are you troubled, and why do doubts arise in your hearts?
24.39: Look at my hands and my feet, it is I. Touch me and see that a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you see I have.»
24.40: Having spoken thus, he showed them his hands and feet.
24.41: As in their joy they still hesitated to believe and could not recover from their astonishment, he said to them, "Do you have anything here to eat?"«
24.42: They presented him with a piece of roasted fish and a honeycomb.
24.43: He took them and ate them in front of them.
24.44: Then he said to them, «This is what I told you while I was still with you: Everything must be fulfilled that is written about me in the Law of Moses, the Prophets and the Psalms.»
24.45: Then he opened their minds to understand the Scriptures,
24.46: And he said to them, «Thus it is written: ‘And thus the Christ had to suffer, and on the third day rise from the dead,’”,
24.47: and that repentance and forgiveness of sins be preached in his name to all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
24.48: You are witnesses to these things.
24.49: »I am going to send you the gift that my Father promised; and you, stay in the city until you are clothed with power from on high.”
24.50: Then he led them out of the city, as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them.
24.51: While he was blessing them, he parted from them and was taken up into heaven.
24.52: For them, after worshipping him, they returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
24.53: And they were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God.
